last_item: Band-2.5 (resource & config safety) FINAL item: APPLY host-sized tuning — UI surface. Read-only GET /api/tuning (Manager.CurrentTuning) + a 'Database tuning' card on Settings showing detected RAM/CPU, the live applied settings, and a per-profile preview labeled by effect. No restart-trigger surface (preview only).
last_result: done (iter 31) — provisioning is CLI-driven, so the Provision result's recommended_tuning/tuning fields were only logged, never surfaced. Added a READ-ONLY surface. Backend: Manager.CurrentTuning(ctx) in internal/pg/tuning_status.go returns detected RAM/CPU, the live applied settings (read via the existing readTunableSettings from pg_settings, normalised to whole MB), and the host-sized recommendation per profile (oltp/mixed/olap via pure RecommendTuning through the hostTuning seam). BEST-EFFORT: if Postgres is unreachable it returns Applied=nil with NO error so the recommendations still load. handleGetTuning wired at GET /api/tuning (read-only; never mutates PG). Frontend: 'Database tuning (host-sized)' card on Settings (web/src/views/DatabaseTuning.tsx) shows the box's RAM/CPU + active profile, the applied settings (each plainly described), and a profile selector that PREVIEWS what each profile would size the box to, every profile labeled by effect; a non-default selection clearly says it's a preview that changes nothing and that a real switch needs a brief Postgres restart (so it's an install/provision-time action, not a panel button — security tie-break: NO restart-trigger surface in the panel). Tests: TestCurrentTuning_SurfacesAppliedAndAllProfiles + TestCurrentTuning_DegradesWhenPostgresUnreachable; DatabaseTuning.test.tsx (mbLabel GB/MB units, host+active render, applied values, null-applied calm warn with recommendations still shown, profile preview describes effect + asserts the no-change copy). Full Go gate (gofmt/vet/test/build) + web gate (typecheck/build/47 vitest tests) green. Reviewed (feature-dev:code-reviewer): no blocking issues; tidied one redundant hostTuning(Mixed) detection call to reuse profiles[1]. BAND 2.5 COMPLETE.
notes: BANDS 1.5 + 2 + 2.5 COMPLETE. NEXT IS BAND 3 (usability): top open items — (a) provision flow shows computed best-defaults up front with each override labeled by effect, safe to accept blindly; (b) every destructive confirm dialog states exactly what happens + what's irreversible; (c) PgBouncer opt-in pooler toggle (DEFAULTS.md pool math, off by default, shows app connection string). NOTE the tuning UI just shipped is read-only/preview; if a real in-panel profile-switch is ever wanted it MUST funnel through ApplyTuning's restartWithRollback + a confirm dialog stating the brief restart (fits band-3 destructive-confirm item) — but it is NOT load-bearing for the north star, so only add it if usability work surfaces a genuine need. DONE iter 31: APPLY host-sized tuning UI SURFACE — read-only GET /api/tuning (Manager.CurrentTuning, internal/pg/tuning_status.go) returns detected RAM/CPU + live applied settings (from pg_settings, whole MB, Applied=nil best-effort when PG unreachable) + per-profile host-sized recommendation; 'Database tuning' card on Settings (web/src/views/DatabaseTuning.tsx) shows applied settings + a per-profile PREVIEW labeled by effect, with copy making clear a non-default selection changes nothing and a real switch needs a restart (no restart-trigger surface). DONE iter 30: APPLY host-sized tuning REAL-PG INTEGRATION TEST (internal/pg/tuning_apply_integration_test.go, //go:build integration, INDIEPG_PG_BINDIR-gated). Proves ApplyTuning end-to-end on a throwaway PG14 cluster: settings land in pg_settings (bytes via unit col, max_connections exact), 2nd run no-op, an unstartable max_connections=1 rolls back via restartWithRollback with PG up -> CodeSafety. tuningTestRunner translates the exact `systemctl restart postgresql` -> pg_ctl restart. INTEGRATION-TEST GOTCHAS: force a deterministic boot failure with a CROSS-GUC constraint (max_connections=1), NOT an oversized memory GUC (overcommit makes it hang); and pg_ctl start/restart needs -l <logfile> or the daemonized postmaster holds the captured stdout pipe and cmd.Run hangs forever. ONLY REMAINING band-2.5 item: the ApplyTuning UI SURFACE — show the applied host-sized defaults (Provision already returns recommended_tuning + tuning status) + the workload-profile override labeled by effect (oltp/olap/mixed); vitest/RTL test; off-the-default profile clearly described before it changes sizing. After that, band 2.5 is complete -> band 3 (usability). DONE iter 29: APPLY host-sized tuning at provision — Manager.ApplyTuning (internal/pg/tuning_apply.go) persists RecommendTuning's five settings via ALTER SYSTEM, idempotent vs pg_settings (bytes-normalised through the unit col), restart-requiring (shared_buffers/max_connections) funneled through restartWithRollback, reloadable via pg_reload_conf. Wired into Provision (replaces iter-28 'surfaced, not applied'); a CodeSafety rollback is tolerated gracefully (provision still succeeds on prior good config). detectTuning seam added for deterministic provision tests. Fully unit-tested. THE TWO NEW TOP BAND-2.5 ITEMS are the apply follow-ups: (a) a real-PG //go:build integration test proving settings land in pg_settings, a 2nd run no-ops, and an oversized shared_buffers rolls back with PG up; (b) the UI surface showing the applied defaults (Provision already returns recommended_tuning + tuning status) + the workload-profile override labeled by effect. ANY future config-apply/override flow MUST funnel its restart through restartWithRollback (snapshot before write) — ApplyTuning is the worked example. DONE iter 28: host-sized tuning PURE SIZING FUNCTION — RecommendTuning(memoryMB, cpuCount, profile) in internal/pg/tuning.go ports the sm tuning.py math (DEFAULTS.md) for shared_buffers/effective_cache_size/work_mem/maintenance_work_mem/max_connections; WorkloadProfile selectable, empty->Mixed default, unknown->ValidationError; total/panic-free on degenerate input. Tested vs sample RAM 1GB-128GB x all 3 profiles + clamp/ordering/degenerate/parse/SettingsMap. DONE iter 27: connection-saturation alert CRITICAL tier connections-critical (pg.connections_percent >= 95%, For 1m, Cooldown 15m, Critical) escalating the existing connections-near-max WARNING (85%); rule-only, metric already real (sampler.go pg_stat_activity / max_connections), ID-based seed auto-adds on upgrade; TestConnectionSaturationTiers proves both tiers fire non-vacuously. DONE iter 26: SELF-HEALING config primitive (internal/pg/safeconfig.go): snapshotAutoConf captures <data_directory>/postgresql.auto.conf BEFORE a restart-requiring change (fails closed if unreadable); restartWithRollback restarts PG and on non-zero `systemctl restart` exit restores the snapshot via `tee` as the postgres user (keeps owner+0600; works while PG is down, unlike ALTER SYSTEM RESET) + restarts again so PG is never left down -> CodeSafety ('rolled back; PG running') or CodeInternal ('PG down'+hint). ANY future config-apply/override flow MUST funnel its restart through restartWithRollback (snapshot before write) — this is exactly what the new APPLY item above must do. DONE iter 25: disk-headroom-low early-warning rule (WARNING 80% host.disk_percent) below critical disk-almost-full (90%). KEY: adding a disk/conn alert is usually RULE-ONLY — Snapshot already has the fields, alert/metrics.go metricValue computes the percent, and background.go's seedDefaultAlertRules inserts only missing IDs (idempotent) so a new DefaultRules() entry auto-seeds on upgrade. Test pattern: extend TestDefaultRules want-list + a focused engine test; background_test asserts len(DefaultRules()) dynamically. --- Alert plumbing: Collector.SampleOnce -> Engine.Evaluate -> dispatchAlertEvents (background.go); metricValue (alert/metrics.go) reads Snapshot fields; go is a snap -> run go/psql/pg_ctl sandbox-disabled; empty $TMPDIR in some shells -> use /tmp. --- HISTORICAL: band 1.5 (backups proven-restorable, S3 nudge, restore-test verify+deep, scheduled jobs) and band 2 (web silent-failure audit, idempotent provisioning, query-box auto-LIMIT+statement_timeout enforced) all COMPLETE. See git log.
north_star: An indie hacker can stand up and run a rock-solid Postgres entirely from the indiepg UI, on best defaults, with safe optional overrides.
